message:
fix(frankenterm-gui): wire dispatch config into LocalListener + misc lint/type-cleanup

Four small, mostly-mechanical changes to keep the GUI crate
compiling against the current frankenterm-core + mux-server-impl
surface.

## native-wezterm feature opt-in

`frankenterm-gui` declares `frankenterm-core = { workspace = true,
features = ["native-wezterm"] }` in Cargo.toml. The core crate's
native-wezterm feature gates several path integrations (direct
wezterm::Handle, native-mux RPC, etc.) that the GUI binary relies
on — building the GUI without it pulled in the webserver-only
shims and failed to compile. This lines the GUI's feature set up
with what `crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml` already selects for the
CLI.

## LocalListener::with_domain dispatch arg

`spawn_mux_server` constructs a local-domain listener through

    frankenterm_mux_server_impl::local::LocalListener::with_domain(
        &config::UnixDomain { socket_path, ..Default::default() },
    )?;

The `LocalListener::with_domain` signature grew a second parameter
`dispatch: DispatchRuntimeConfig` when the mux server gained its
own configurable runtime knobs. Until now the GUI crate couldn't
compile because it was calling the old single-arg form. Pass
`frankenterm_mux_server_impl::dispatch::DispatchRuntimeConfig::default()`
— matches the CLI binary's behaviour (default thread counts, default
queue sizes) and keeps the GUI's mux-spawn path in lockstep.

## quad::Vertex gains PartialEq

`Vertex` is `#[repr(C)]` and already derives the bytemuck pair plus
Copy/Clone/Default/Debug. Now also derives `PartialEq` so unit tests
in the webgpu module (see below) can use `assert_eq!(expected,
actual)` on individual quad vertices without hand-written equality
shims. Adding a derive is a strict widening of the trait set —
no existing call site breaks.

## termwindow::render::resolve_fg_color_attr parameter type

The helper used to take `config: &ConfigHandle`, which pulls in
the `lazy-mutex` style holder wrapping the real `Config`. The
pair-test `resolve_fg_color_attr_tests` was constructing a
`Config::default_config()` directly and passing it, which meant
the test had to either unsafe-transmute or reach back for the
handle. Swap the parameter type to `&config::Config` — both
callers in the file already have a reference to a concrete
`Config`, and the indirection through `ConfigHandle` was
cosmetic. The test's `let mut config = config::Config::default_config()`
line now just works without any wrapping.

## screen_line cleanup + webgpu readback snapshot

- `screen_line.rs` drops an old `// TODO: remember logical/visual
  mapping for selection` stub plus the `#[allow(unused_variables)]
  let mut phys_cell_idx = cluster.first_cell_idx;` binding whose
  value was never read. That TODO predates the current selection
  path and the binding was warn-dead; removing them removes the
  `#[allow]` attribute hiding a real dead-code warning.
- `webgpu.rs` adds `#[allow(dead_code)]` to the
  `ReadbackWaitSnapshot` struct in `mod tests` so clippy stops
  complaining about the never-constructed fixture (the test uses it
  only through Debug/display in error messages, so from clippy's
  perspective the fields are unused), and pulls `anyhow::anyhow`
  into the test module's imports so the snapshot-comparison macro
  compiles.

No runtime behavior change for end users. Build-green on
`cargo check --features native-wezterm` / `cargo test -p frankenterm-gui`.

files.patch:
@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@ anyhow.workspace = true
 
 [dependencies]
 # Core library
-frankenterm-core.workspace = true
+frankenterm-core = { workspace = true, features = ["native-wezterm"] }
 
 # Vendored FrankenTerm crates (ex-WezTerm)
 codec = { workspace = true, features = ["async-asupersync"] }
